title: 7 Best Online Course Platforms in 2026: Create, Sell & Scale Your Knowledge Business
content:
# 7 Best Online Course Platforms in 2026: Create, Sell & Scale Your Knowledge Business

*Affiliate disclosure: This article contains affiliate links. If you purchase through our links, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. This does not influence our recommendations.*

**Last updated: March 2, 2026**

## Quick Answer

**Thinkific is the best online course platform in 2026 for most creators**, offering unlimited courses on all paid plans, zero transaction fees, a generous free tier, and the most flexible course builder — all starting at $49/month. It's the platform that gives you the most control without the complexity of Kajabi or the revenue share of Udemy. For creators who want an all-in-one business platform (courses + email + funnels + website + community), **Kajabi** justifies its $149/month price by replacing 5-6 separate tools. For maximum audience reach without upfront investment, **Udemy** provides access to 70+ million learners — you sacrifice pricing control and margins, but gain immediate distribution that self-hosted platforms can't match. The online education market is projected to reach $462 billion by 2027, and these platforms make it possible for anyone with expertise to build a scalable knowledge business.

### Quick Picks

- **Best Overall:** Thinkific — Zero transaction fees + unlimited courses + best free plan
- **Best All-in-One:** Kajabi — Courses + email + funnels + website + community in one
- **Best for Reach:** Udemy — 70M+ learners, no upfront cost

---

## Comparison Table: All 7 Online Course Platforms at a Glance

| Rank | Platform | Starting Price | Free Plan | Transaction Fee | Built-in Marketing | Best For |
|------|----------|---------------|-----------|----------------|-------------------|----------|
| #1 | Thinkific | $49/mo | Yes | 0% | Basic | Best overall |
| #2 | Kajabi | $149/mo | No | 0% | Full suite | All-in-one |
| #3 | Teachable | $39/mo | No | 5% (Basic) | Basic | Simplicity |
| #4 | Podia | $39/mo | No | 5% (Mover) | Email included | Digital products |
| #5 | Udemy | Free | N/A | 3-75% revenue share | Marketplace | Maximum reach |
| #6 | Skillshare | Free to teach | N/A | Royalty model | Marketplace | Creative courses |
| #7 | Mighty Networks | $41/mo | No | 0% | Community-first | Communities |

---

## Individual Reviews

### #1. Thinkific — Best Overall Course Platform

Thinkific earns the top spot by offering the most creator-friendly combination of features, pricing, and flexibility — zero transaction fees on all plans, unlimited courses, and a genuine free tier that lets you validate your course idea before spending a dollar.

| Spec | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Free Plan | 1 course, unlimited students, no transaction fee |
| Basic | $49/mo — unlimited courses, coupons, affiliate selling |
| Start | $99/mo — payment plans, subscriptions, bulk selling |
| Grow | $199/mo — API access, priority support, advanced tools |
| Transaction Fee | 0% on all plans |
| Payment Processing | Stripe + PayPal |
| Mobile App | Branded iOS + Android app (Start plan+) |

**Pros:**
- **Zero transaction fees on every plan** including the free tier — Thinkific takes nothing from your revenue. Teachable charges 5% on its Basic plan, Podia charges 5% on Mover, and Udemy takes 3-75% depending on the sale source. Over $50K in annual course revenue, that fee difference saves $2,500-37,500
- **Most flexible course builder** with drag-and-drop lessons, multimedia content types (video, audio, PDF, text, quizzes, surveys, assignments), drip scheduling, prerequisite logic, and completion certificates — more structural control than any competitor
- **Free plan is genuinely useful** — create 1 course with unlimited students, no transaction fees, and basic course completion pages. Enough to validate a course concept and make your first sales before upgrading
- **Branded mobile app** on Start plan ($99/month) puts your courses in a custom iOS and Android app under your brand — students download YOUR app, not a generic course player
- **Built-in community** (Thinkific Spaces) provides discussion boards, live events, and member areas alongside your courses — reducing the need for a separate community platform like Circle or Mighty Networks

**Cons:**
- **No built-in email marketing or funnel builder** — Thinkific handles courses and payments but requires external tools (Mailchimp, Kit, ActiveCampaign) for email sequences and sales funnels. Kajabi includes all of this natively
- Marketing features are basic compared to Kajabi — no sales pipeline management, no automated webinar funnels, no built-in affiliate tracking on the Basic plan
- **$49/month starting price** (for meaningful features) is higher than Teachable's $39/month or Podia's $39/month — though the zero transaction fee offsets this at higher revenue levels

**Who it's for:** Course creators who want maximum control over their course experience with zero transaction fees and don't mind connecting external tools for marketing. If you're selling $10K+/year in courses, Thinkific's fee-free model saves more than enough to justify the monthly price.

👉 [**Try Thinkific Free — Then $49/month**](https://thinkific.com/)

---

### #2. Kajabi — Best All-in-One Business Platform

Kajabi claims second place as the only platform that replaces your course hosting, email marketing, sales funnels, website builder, blog, and community platform with a single subscription — eliminating tool sprawl at a premium price.

| Spec | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Kickstarter | $71/mo (annual) — 1 product, 1,000 contacts |
| Basic | $149/mo — 3 products, 10,000 contacts, 1,000 active members |
| Growth | $199/mo — 15 products, 25,000 contacts, 10,000 active members |
| Pro | $399/mo — 100 products, 100,000 contacts, 25,000 members |
| Transaction Fee | 0% on all plans |
| Email Marketing | Built-in (sequences, broadcasts, automations) |
| Sales Funnels | Built-in (Pipelines) with templates |

**Pros:**
- **True all-in-one platform** replaces 5-6 separate tools — course hosting (Thinkific), email marketing (Mailchimp), sales funnels (ClickFunnels), website builder (Squarespace), blog (WordPress), and community (Circle). At $149/month, Kajabi costs less than subscribing to all these separately
- **Pipelines (sales funnels)** with pre-built templates for webinar funnels, product launches, lead magnets, and evergreen courses — Kajabi is the only course platform where you can build complete sales funnels without touching a third-party tool
- **iOS and Android mobile app** for students comes standard — your students get a polished app experience with offline access, push notifications, and community features under your brand
- **24/7 live chat support** and the most active creator community (Kajabi Heroes) provide real-time help and peer learning — the support ecosystem is meaningfully better than competitors
- **Kajabi University** provides free training on course creation, marketing, and scaling — essentially a course on how to build a successful course business, included with your subscription

**Cons:**
- **$149/month minimum for usable features** makes Kajabi the most expensive platform on this list — 3x Thinkific's starting price and nearly 4x Teachable's. At $1,788/year, you need substantial revenue to justify the investment
- **3-product limit on Basic plan** is restrictive — creators with multiple courses, coaching programs, and digital downloads will quickly need to upgrade to Growth ($199/month)
- Course builder is less flexible than Thinkific — fewer content types, less structural customization, and more opinionated templates. Kajabi prioritizes marketing over course design

**Who it's for:** Established course creators earning $5K+/month who want to consolidate their entire tech stack into one platform. If you're currently paying for course hosting + email + funnels + website separately, Kajabi likely costs less while simplifying your operations.

👉 [**Try Kajabi — Starting at $149/month**](https://kajabi.com/)

---

### #3. Teachable — Best for Simplicity

Teachable takes third place for offering the simplest course creation experience available — from signup to published course in under an hour, with a learning curve so gentle that non-technical creators thrive.

| Spec | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Basic | $39/mo — unlimited courses, 5% transaction fee |
| Pro | $99/mo — 0% transaction fee, graded quizzes, certificates |
| Pro+ | $199/mo — custom user roles, advanced reporting |
| Business | $665/mo — bulk enrollments, custom code |
| Payment Processing | Teachable Payments (Stripe-based) |
| Coaching | Built-in coaching product type |
| Affiliates | Pro plan and above |

**Pros:**
- **Simplest course creation workflow** — Teachable's step-by-step course builder guides you through curriculum design, content upload, pricing, and publishing with minimal decisions. First-time course creators consistently report going from zero to published in 2-4 hours
- **Built-in coaching product type** lets you sell 1-on-1 or group coaching alongside courses — with scheduled milestones, task assignments, and progress tracking. Thinkific and Kajabi require workarounds for coaching delivery
- **Teachable Payments** handles all payment processing, tax collection, and affiliate payouts in 130+ countries — you receive one payment, and Teachable handles the rest. Less control but dramatically simpler than managing your own Stripe account
- **Certificates and graded quizzes** on Pro plan provide verifiable course completion credentials — important for professional development courses where students need proof of learning
- **Mobile-optimized student experience** works perfectly on phones and tablets without a dedicated app — responsive design ensures your course looks professional on any device

**Cons:**
- **5% transaction fee on the Basic plan** eats directly into your revenue — on $50,000 in annual sales, that's $2,500 going to Teachable on top of your $468 annual subscription. The Pro plan ($99/month) eliminates the fee but costs 2.5x more
- **No free plan** — Teachable discontinued its free tier, meaning you must commit to $39/month minimum before making your first sale. Thinkific's free plan removes this risk entirely
- Marketing features are minimal — no email marketing, no sales funnels, no webinar hosting. You'll need external tools for everything beyond basic sales pages and checkout

**Who it's for:** First-time course creators who want the fastest path from idea to published course with minimal technical complexity. If you're a coach, consultant, or expert who wants to get your knowledge online quickly without learning a complex platform, Teachable removes the friction.

👉 [**Try Teachable — Starting at $39/month**](https://teachable.com/)

---

### #4. Podia — Best for Multi-Product Creators

Podia earns fourth place as the most versatile platform for creators who sell multiple product types — courses, digital downloads, coaching, webinars, and community memberships — all from a single, clean dashboard.

| Spec | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Mover | $39/mo ($33/mo annual) — unlimited products, 5% transaction fee |
| Shaker | $89/mo ($75/mo annual) — 0% fee, affiliates, PayPal |
| Earthquaker | $199/mo — priority support, onboarding call |
| Products | Courses, downloads, coaching, webinars, community |
| Email Marketing | Built-in on all plans |
| Website | Full website builder included |

**Pros:**
- **Sell every digital product type from one platform** — online courses, digital downloads (ebooks, templates, PDFs), coaching sessions (1-on-1 or group), webinars (live or pre-recorded), and community memberships. No other platform at this price offers this product variety
- **Built-in email marketing on all plans** including basic automation, broadcast emails, and subscriber management — eliminates the need for a separate Mailchimp or Kit subscription
- **Full website builder** creates a professional storefront with custom pages, blog, testimonials, and product showcase — your entire online presence in one tool
- **Clean, clutter-free interface** is consistently praised in reviews — Podia strips away enterprise complexity and presents a simple dashboard that solo creators can navigate without tutorials
- **Bundling** lets you package multiple products (course + ebook + coaching) into a single offer with one checkout — a proven strategy for increasing average order value

**Cons:**
- **5% transaction fee on Mover plan** means you're paying $39/month AND 5% of every sale. A creator earning $3,000/month pays $189/month total ($39 + $150 in fees) — making Podia more expensive than Thinkific's $49/month with 0% fees
- Course builder is basic compared to Thinkific and Kajabi — limited quiz types, no prerequisite logic, no completion certificates, and fewer content options
- No marketplace or discovery — unlike Udemy or Skillshare, Podia provides no built-in audience. All traffic must come from your own marketing efforts

**Who it's for:** Solo creators selling multiple types of digital products who want everything in one simple platform. If you sell a course AND an ebook AND coaching AND a community membership, Podia's unified dashboard beats managing separate tools for each product type.

👉 [**Try Podia — Starting at $39/month**](https://podia.com/)

---

### #5. Udemy — Best for Maximum Reach

Udemy takes fifth place as the world's largest online course marketplace — 70+ million learners, 220,000+ courses, and built-in distribution that no self-hosted platform can match. You sacrifice control for reach.

| Spec | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Price to Create | Free |
| Revenue Share (Instructor Coupon) | 97% to instructor (3% to Udemy) |
| Revenue Share (Organic Search) | 50% to instructor |
| Revenue Share (Udemy Ads) | 25% to instructor |
| Learner Base | 70+ million registered students |
| Course Discovery | Udemy marketplace search + recommendations |
| Pricing Control | Limited ($19.99-$199.99 range) |

**Pros:**
- **70+ million registered learners** provides immediate access to an audience you'd spend years and thousands of dollars building independently — publish a well-optimized course and Udemy's search algorithm surfaces it to relevant students automatically
- **Zero upfront cost** — creating and publishing a course on Udemy is completely free. No monthly subscription, no setup fees, no risk. You invest time creating content and Udemy handles hosting, payments, and delivery
- **97% revenue share on instructor-promoted sales** means courses you sell through your own marketing (direct links, coupons) generate nearly full revenue — the 3% fee barely covers payment processing
- **Built-in SEO and recommendation engine** places your course alongside related content — students searching for topics in your niche discover your course through Udemy's internal search, category browsing, and AI-powered recommendations
- **Credibility through association** — being on Udemy provides social proof. Students trust the platform's review system, refund policy, and quality standards, lowering the barrier to purchase for unknown instructors

**Cons:**
- **Udemy controls pricing** — courses are frequently discounted to $9.99-$14.99 during sales events (which happen almost monthly), regardless of your intended price. A $199 premium course is regularly sold for $12, devaluing your expertise
- **50-75% revenue share on organic and ad-driven sales** means Udemy keeps the majority of revenue from students who find your course through the marketplace — the platform benefits from your content more than you do
- **No student email access** — Udemy doesn't share student email addresses, preventing you from building a direct relationship with your audience. You can't email students outside the platform or market other products to them

**Who it's for:** Subject matter experts who want to reach the largest possible audience without building a marketing machine. First-time instructors testing course viability, professionals building credibility in their field, and creators who prefer trading control for distribution.

👉 [**Create a Course on Udemy — Free to Start**](https://udemy.com/)

---

### #6. Skillshare — Best for Creative Courses

Skillshare earns sixth place as the premier marketplace for creative and skills-based education — illustration, photography, design, writing, music production, and entrepreneurship thrive in Skillshare's project-based learning environment.

| Spec | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Price to Teach | Free (approval required) |
| Revenue Model | Minutes watched (royalty pool) |
| Student Base | Millions of premium members |
| Course Format | Short-form (30-60 min) + class projects |
| Categories | Creative, Business, Technology, Lifestyle |
| Referral Bonus | $10 per new premium member referred |

**Pros:**
- **34,000+ courses focused on creative skills** make Skillshare the strongest platform for design, illustration, photography, video production, writing, music, and creative entrepreneurship — students specifically seek out creative education here
- **Short-form course format** (30-60 minutes with class projects) lowers the barrier to course creation — you don't need to produce a 20-hour comprehensive curriculum. Focused, project-based classes perform best
- **Passive income potential** through the royalty pool — once published, your course earns money every time a premium member watches your content, indefinitely. Popular instructors report earning $1,000-10,000+/month passively
- **Built-in audience of millions** of premium subscribers actively browsing for new courses — no marketing required to get initial views, though promotion significantly boosts earnings
- **$10 referral bonus per new premium member** creates an additional revenue stream — promote Skillshare through your social channels and earn for every signup, regardless of whether they watch your course

**Cons:**
- **No control over pricing** — students pay Skillshare a subscription fee, and you receive royalties based on minutes watched. You can't set a course price, offer discounts, or create premium tiers
- Revenue per student is low — individual course earnings often amount to $0.05-0.50 per student per month, requiring thousands of viewers to generate meaningful income
- **Course approval process** means not every instructor is accepted — Skillshare reviews sample content before allowing you to publish, and rejection means wasted effort

**Who it's for:** Creative professionals who can teach practical, visual skills in short-form format and want passive income from a built-in audience. If you're an illustrator, photographer, designer, or creative entrepreneur, Skillshare's audience is specifically looking for what you teach.

👉 [**Start Teaching on Skillshare — Free to Apply**](https://skillshare.com/)

---

### #7. Mighty Networks — Best for Community-First Courses

Mighty Networks rounds out our list as the platform that puts community at the center of learning — courses are embedded within a community experience, not the other way around. Members interact, discuss, and learn together.

| Spec | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Community Plan | $41/mo — unlimited members, events, discussions |
| Business Plan | $119/mo — courses + community + AI features |
| Path-to-Pro | $219/mo — advanced analytics + white-label app |
| Transaction Fee | 0% on all plans |
| AI Features | Course outlines, content improvement, member matching |
| Mobile App | Branded iOS + Android |

**Pros:**
- **Community-first architecture** means courses live inside a vibrant member community — discussions, live events, member profiles, and peer connections create an experience that standalone course platforms can't replicate
- **AI-powered course creation** generates course outlines, improves content drafts, surfaces discussion questions, and auto-introduces members with shared interests — reducing the time from idea to published course
- **Native livestreaming** with high-velocity chat and multi-speaker view — host live workshops, Q&A sessions, and community events without Zoom, YouTube Live, or third-party streaming tools
- **Branded mobile app** on Path-to-Pro ($219/month) gives your community and courses a dedicated app under your brand — members download YOUR app from the App Store
- **Zero transaction fees** on all plans — Mighty Networks takes nothing from your course sales, membership fees, or event ticket revenue

**Cons:**
- **Courses require Business plan ($119/month)** — the Community plan ($41/month) doesn't include course hosting. If you specifically want to sell courses, Mighty Networks is 2.4x more expensive than Thinkific's starting plan
- Course builder is secondary to community features — the learning experience is functional but less sophisticated than Thinkific's or Teachable's dedicated course builders
- Smaller ecosystem — fewer integrations, smaller knowledge base, and less creator education compared to established platforms like Kajabi or Thinkific

**Who it's for:** Creators who believe learning happens best in community — coaches, membership operators, cohort-based course creators, and community leaders who want courses as a component of a larger member experience, not the other way around.

👉 [**Try Mighty Networks — Starting at $41/month**](https://mightynetworks.com/)

---

## How These Platforms Were Chosen

These rankings synthesize data from [Blogging Wizard](https://bloggingwizard.com/online-course-platforms/), [Learning Revolution](https://www.learningrevolution.net/best-online-course-platforms/), [Kindlepreneur](https://kindlepreneur.com/thinkific-vs-teachable/), and direct platform testing, along with aggregate creator reviews from Reddit (r/coursecreation, r/Entrepreneur), Trustpilot, and G2.

**Selection criteria weighted for 2026 use:**

1. **Course creation features (25%)** — Content types, quiz options, drip scheduling, certificates, and student experience quality
2. **Pricing & value (25%)** — Monthly cost, transaction fees, and total cost at $10K, $50K, and $100K annual revenue levels
3. **Marketing tools (20%)** — Email marketing, sales funnels, affiliate programs, and promotional capabilities
4. **Scalability (15%)** — Product limits, student capacity, and ability to grow from first course to full business
5. **Ease of use (10%)** — Time from signup to published course, learning curve, and support quality
6. **Community & engagement (5%)** — Discussion forums, live events, and student interaction features

---

## Buyer's Guide: Choosing a Course Platform in 2026

### Self-Hosted vs. Marketplace
Self-hosted platforms (Thinkific, Kajabi, Teachable) give you full control over pricing, branding, and student relationships — but require you to drive all your own traffic. Marketplaces (Udemy, Skillshare) provide built-in audiences — but take significant revenue share and limit your control. Most successful creators use both: a marketplace course for audience discovery and a self-hosted platform for premium offerings.

### Calculate Your True Cost
Don't compare monthly prices alone. Factor in transaction fees, payment processing fees (typically 2.9% + $0.30 via Stripe), and the cost of external tools you'll need. Thinkific at $49/month with 0% fees costs less than Teachable at $39/month with 5% fees once you hit $200/month in revenue.

### Start Small, Scale Deliberately
You don't need Kajabi's $149/month all-in-one suite to launch your first course. Start with Thinkific's free plan, validate your course idea, make your first sales, then upgrade as revenue justifies the investment.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is the best online course platform in 2026?
**Thinkific is the best overall course platform in 2026**, offering zero transaction fees, unlimited courses on paid plans, a genuine free tier, and the most flexible course builder. For all-in-one business needs, Kajabi ($149/month) is best. For maximum audience reach, Udemy is free to start.

### Can you really make money selling online courses?
Yes. The global e-learning market is projected to reach $462 billion by 2027. Successful course creators on Thinkific and Kajabi routinely earn $5,000-50,000+/month. However, the majority of courses on marketplaces like Udemy earn under $1,000/year. Success requires quality content + effective marketing.

### Thinkific vs Kajabi: which is better?
**Thinkific** if you want the best course builder with zero fees and are comfortable using external tools for email and funnels. **Kajabi** if you want everything (courses + email + funnels + website + community) in one platform and can justify $149/month.

### Is Udemy worth it for course creators?
Udemy provides unmatched audience access (70M+ learners) at zero upfront cost. However, aggressive discounting (courses regularly sold at $9.99) and 50-75% revenue share on organic sales make it a challenging primary income source. Best used for audience building alongside a self-hosted platform.

### How much does it cost to create an online course?
Platform costs range from $0 (Udemy, Thinkific free) to $149+/month (Kajabi). Production costs depend on quality: webcam recordings cost $0, while professional video production runs $2,000-10,000+. Most successful courses are created with $100-500 in equipment (microphone, basic lighting, screen recording software).

### Which course platform has no transaction fees?
Thinkific, Kajabi, and Mighty Networks charge 0% transaction fees on all plans. Teachable charges 5% on Basic. Podia charges 5% on Mover. Udemy takes 3-75% depending on the sale source.

### Do I need a website to sell online courses?
Not necessarily. Kajabi, Podia, and Thinkific include website builders. Udemy and Skillshare host everything on their marketplace. However, having your own website provides SEO benefits, brand credibility, and email capture that marketplace-only presence doesn't.

### How do I choose between a marketplace and self-hosted platform?
Use a marketplace (Udemy, Skillshare) for audience discovery and social proof. Use a self-hosted platform (Thinkific, Kajabi) for premium pricing, student ownership, and brand control. Most successful creators use both.

---

## Conclusion: Our Top Pick for 2026

After comparing course creation features, calculating total cost of ownership at multiple revenue levels, testing marketing capabilities, and evaluating student experience quality, **Thinkific is the best online course platform for most creators in 2026**.

It doesn't have the most marketing tools (Kajabi), the simplest interface (Teachable), or the largest audience (Udemy). But Thinkific delivers the best **combination** of course-building flexibility, zero transaction fees, free plan for validation, and scalability from first course to full business.

**Here's the decision simplified:**

- **Best overall?** → [Thinkific](https://thinkific.com/) — $49/mo, 0% fees, unlimited courses
- **Want all-in-one?** → [Kajabi](https://kajabi.com/) — $149/mo, courses + email + funnels + website
- **Simplest to start?** → [Teachable](https://teachable.com/) — $39/mo, publish in hours
- **Sell multiple product types?** → [Podia](https://podia.com/) — $39/mo, courses + downloads + coaching
- **Maximum audience reach?** → [Udemy](https://udemy.com/) — Free, 70M+ learners
- **Creative skills?** → [Skillshare](https://skillshare.com/) — Free to teach, passive royalties
- **Community-first learning?** → [Mighty Networks](https://mightynetworks.com/) — $119/mo, courses + community

Your expertise has value. The platform you choose determines how efficiently you can package, sell, and deliver that value. Pick the one that matches your current stage — and remember, you can always migrate as your business grows.

---

*Last updated: March 2, 2026. Prices are subject to change based on promotions and plan duration. Revenue share percentages reflect current terms at time of publication. Feature availability varies by plan tier — verify current offerings on each provider's pricing page.*
